writing.notes: Good. The Cato chapter is the best thing I've written in this book so far. Something clicked about the difference between performing discipline and actually having it — Cato had no audience for most of what he did. That's the thing.
writing.breakthrough: The book's argument is becoming clearer: discipline isn't about doing hard things, it's about doing the right things consistently regardless of whether anyone is watching. The chapter structure may need to change to reflect this.

bookstore.notes: The banned books event was genuinely moving. Families came out; people were angry in a good way. A woman brought her daughter who had been told at school not to read The Kite Runner. They both cried a little. The store is doing what it should be doing.
bookstore.financial: Week looked better. Two strong days off the event traffic.

family.notes: Liam asked me what the books we gave away were about. I tried to explain banned books to a six-year-old. He said 'so someone said you can't read them and you just read them anyway?' I said yes. He said 'that's what you always say. Do the obstacle.' He's six.

daily_stoic_newsletter: Sent. On Cato — used material from today's writing session.
reading: Plutarch's Lives — the Cato section. Using primary sources directly now.
blockers: Book structure still in flux — may need to reorder chapters
concern: I'm writing faster than I'm thinking. Need to slow down and let the argument catch up to the words.
